title: Them Robot

jokes.text: Power's out. Oh, your father must be sleeping on his side again.
jokes.setup: Standard response to power outage - looking for explanation
jokes.punchline: Homer's sleep position somehow causes power outages
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurd domestic observation. Good character-specific humor about Homer's physical impact on infrastructure.

jokes.text: From downtown! And it counts!
jokes.setup: Homer apparently makes some kind of basketball-style shot
jokes.punchline: Sports announcer enthusiasm for mundane household activity
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag format - treating mundane action as sports achievement.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, you don't want to go to the hospital at 5:00 on a Friday. Whoo-hoo! Thank God it's TGIF!
jokes.setup: Someone appears to be injured and needs medical attention
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizes weekend plans over medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness - genuine healthcare insight twisted through his self-centered lens.

jokes.text: Bad news, Lang-Soo. Some casual Friday this is. You're outta here, drug-o.
jokes.setup: Drug testing announcement at the plant
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately fires someone with casual cruelty and dated slang
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns characterization - cartoonish evil with period-inappropriate slang like 'drug-o'.

jokes.text: I told them to make these track d'oh-proof!
jokes.setup: Homer appears to have fallen or stumbled on some tracks
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about his own catchphrase causing accidents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - the show acknowledging Homer's catchphrase as an in-universe problem. Creative and well-executed.

jokes.text: Yeah, but a weekend without drinking is no big deal. I did it when I was in that alcohol-induced coma.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he can handle not drinking for a weekend
jokes.punchline: His only precedent was being unconscious from alcohol poisoning
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Homer's twisted logic makes his horrific medical emergency seem like a positive precedent.

jokes.text: you promised to help me write a iku. Why did I do that? It sounds so dull and boring. What was I thinking?
jokes.setup: Lisa reminds Homer of his promise to help with haiku
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately regrets and questions his past self's judgment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's complete inability to maintain interest in intellectual pursuits.

jokes.text: Liquor, mustn't think of liquor...
jokes.setup: Homer trying to avoid thinking about alcohol
jokes.punchline: The classic psychology of trying not to think about something
jokes.explanation: Decent character beat showing Homer's weak willpower, but relies on familiar psychological concept.

jokes.text: Hey, there! What's cookin'? Come on, boy, crack my seal and drink your father.
jokes.setup: Homer talking to a bottle of alcohol
jokes.punchline: The bottle talks back and suggests Homer drink it in disturbing familial terms
jokes.explanation: Creepy and funny hallucination sequence. The paternal language is disturbing and effective.

jokes.text: A surprisingly not-horrible fruit drink called a mimosa. There's champagne in those! Then there's champagne in me!
jokes.setup: Homer innocently describing his drink choice
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization and panic about accidental alcohol consumption
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent to panicked. Homer's ignorance about alcohol in common drinks is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, man, I'd better have some coffee and iced tea. No! Irish and Long Island! What about this lemonade? Mike's Hard! Then I better soak up the alcohol with some food! Whoo! Cake! Rum cake! Crepes! Suzette! Cherries! Whoo! Jubilee!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to find non-alcoholic options at brunch
jokes.punchline: Every innocent option contains alcohol, building to frantic realization
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating panic sequence. The rhythm builds perfectly as each 'safe' option is revealed to contain alcohol.

jokes.text: O, Lord, I have once again besotted my liver with Thy fermented gifts. If You can weasel me out of this physical, I will blow Your mind by doing something incredibly holy, at some point. Amen.
jokes.setup: Homer praying for divine intervention in his drug test
jokes.punchline: Completely casual and vague promise to God with no real commitment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific prayer combining religious language with Homer's complete lack of sincerity. 'Blow Your mind' is perfect.

jokes.text: your cells have been overexposed to gamma radiation. Like the Hulk? Well, sort of like the Hulk. But instead of getting powerful, you're going to get very, very sick.
jokes.setup: Homer comparing his radiation exposure to the Hulk's origin
jokes.punchline: Doctor crushes his superhero fantasy with medical reality
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of pop culture reference. Takes Homer's optimistic comparison and delivers harsh reality check.

jokes.text: Hulk smash... Yes, Hulk smash.
jokes.setup: Homer weakly attempting Hulk impression despite being told he's dying
jokes.punchline: Doctor's patronizing agreement to dying man's fantasy
jokes.explanation: Sad follow-up to previous joke. The pathetic delivery and patronizing response add dark humor.

jokes.text: This trapdoor app works like a charm. Excellent.
jokes.setup: Burns apparently disposing of the doctor through a trapdoor
jokes.punchline: Treating medieval execution method as modern technology app
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of old-fashioned villainy with modern tech language. Perfect Burns character moment.

jokes.text: Smithers, it's time to re-staff with the super-intelligent kangaroos we've been breeding! I'm sorry, sir, but they just filled their pouches with office supplies and hopped away. Even the joeys?
jokes.setup: Burns suggesting replacement workers with bred kangaroos
jokes.punchline: The kangaroos have stolen supplies and escaped, including the babies
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd Burns scheme with perfect follow-through. The detail about joeys adds extra layer of failure.

jokes.text: More high-tech than kangaroos?
jokes.setup: Smithers suggesting a more high-tech solution than kangaroos
jokes.punchline: Burns genuinely considers kangaroos high-tech
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns logic - his complete disconnection from reality makes kangaroos seem like cutting-edge technology to him.

jokes.text: Comicon nerds! You fool! These are robots!
jokes.setup: Burns misidentifying the robots as comic convention attendees
jokes.punchline: Smithers corrects his pop culture confusion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' disconnect from modern culture. Decent setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: I give you permission to shake your fists in anger twice.
jokes.setup: Burns announcing workers will be replaced by robots
jokes.punchline: Magnanimously allowing exactly two fist shakes of anger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns dialogue - treating worker rage as something he can meter out in precise quantities. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: I didn't say, Monty Says. Now I have cause to terminate. This is crap. Now get out. You didn't say Monty Says.
jokes.setup: Burns creating arbitrary rules for worker behavior
jokes.punchline: Using children's game Simon Says as legal justification for firing
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of Burns' arbitrary cruelty. Using childhood game as corporate policy is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: This is no game!
jokes.setup: Worker protesting Burns' Simon Says firing policy
jokes.punchline: Ironic protest since it literally is a game
jokes.explanation: Simple irony beat. Works as response but not particularly creative on its own.

jokes.text: So we need one meat sock on the payroll.
jokes.setup: Smithers explaining need for one human worker
jokes.punchline: Referring to human employee as 'meat sock'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dehumanizing term. 'Meat sock' perfectly captures corporate callousness in wonderfully specific language.

jokes.text: One, I liked the new microwave in the break room. Just push 'popcorn,' you don't have to know how long. Two, replacing us with robots is heartless and despicable. Three, how about a farewell party with a caricature artist? You know, it's something both kids and adults can enjoy.
jokes.setup: Worker giving final speech before being fired
jokes.punchline: Mixing trivial praise, legitimate complaint, and odd party request
jokes.explanation: Great structure showing how normal people can't stay focused during crisis. The caricature artist detail is perfectly random.

jokes.text: Does the chair go back like this? Yes. Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer being offered a soul-crushing job with one redeeming feature
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate joy over reclining chair feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - completely ignoring job description to focus on chair comfort. Character-driven and believable.

jokes.text: It's one, excluding mine falsely!
jokes.setup: Smithers suggesting there are two human workers including himself
jokes.punchline: Burns' grammatically confused attempt to exclude Smithers
jokes.explanation: Good Burns speech pattern - his attempt at complex grammar fails awkwardly but characteristically.

jokes.text: I can do that.
jokes.setup: Smithers demonstrating some human skill robots supposedly can't match
jokes.punchline: Robot immediately proves it can replicate the action
jokes.explanation: Standard 'robots are better' gag. Economical but predictable setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Nice parking, idiot.
jokes.setup: Robot apparently demonstrating another human behavior
jokes.punchline: Robots can even replicate human rudeness and road rage
jokes.explanation: Good extension of robot replacement theme - they can even do the worst human behaviors. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: So, workin' hard or hardly workin'? I said, workin' hard or hardly workin'? Workin' hard or hardly working?! Working hard or hardly working?!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make small talk with robots using classic workplace joke
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly frustrated and loud when robots don't respond
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's social awkwardness. The repetition builds frustration effectively.

jokes.text: It's a simple question! Are you A.) Working hard or B.) Ow!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to force response from robots with multiple choice format
jokes.punchline: Gets hurt mid-sentence, probably by robot
jokes.explanation: Good physical punctuation to escalating frustration. The interruption timing works well.

jokes.text: Hey, Homer, you know what I'm playing for ya? The world's smallest violin. And now I got to sell it, just to make my rent!
jokes.setup: Lenny using classic 'world's smallest violin' dismissive gesture
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually has to sell this tiny violin due to poverty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of classic insult. Takes familiar expression and makes it literally true due to economic desperation. Excellent craft.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Where's the bow? I can't sell it without the bow!
jokes.setup: Lenny's tiny violin joke revealed to be real financial necessity
jokes.punchline: Panics about missing accessory that would impact resale value
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of previous joke. The practical concern about violin accessories makes the desperation even funnier.

jokes.text: The buyer clearly specified violin and bow!
jokes.setup: Carl suggesting alternative playing method
jokes.punchline: Lenny reveals specific contractual requirements for tiny violin sale
jokes.explanation: Excellent continuation building the absurdity. The specificity of buyer requirements makes the situation more desperately funny.

jokes.text: Ugh! You call this beer? It's watered-down swill!
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to make himself a drink at Moe's
jokes.punchline: Even self-made beer is terrible, showing how bad things have gotten
jokes.explanation: Standard bar complaint but works to show degraded conditions. Character-appropriate but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: ♪ When the moon hits your eye like a big pizza pie ♪ ♪ That's amore ♪ When you do chair gondola all by yourself, it almost seems stupid.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently doing some kind of romantic gondola chair activity alone
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on the sadness of solo romantic gestures
jokes.explanation: Good melancholy humor. Homer's rare moment of self-awareness about his pathetic situation is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Well, at least it doesn't feel pain. Wait a minute... D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer consoling himself that robot doesn't feel pain
jokes.punchline: Realizes this means the robot can't appreciate his friendship either
jokes.explanation: Good logical reversal. Homer's attempt at optimism backfires when he thinks it through. Well-structured realization.

jokes.text: Will you be my friend? Friendship cannot exist between man and machine. But I can simulate interest in your statements.
jokes.setup: Homer asking robot to be his friend
jokes.punchline: Robot's coldly logical but somewhat accommodating response
jokes.explanation: Good robot characterization balancing harsh logic with minimal accommodation. The phrasing is clinically funny.

jokes.text: Look at what happened to the man on my pizza box.
jokes.setup: Character commenting on town's economic decline
jokes.punchline: Pizza box mascot apparently shows signs of economic hardship
jokes.explanation: Creative visual gag concept. Using commercial imagery to show economic impact is clever and economical.

jokes.text: Guess I'll just have to become beautiful on the inside. Which he didn't.
jokes.setup: Character resolving to develop inner beauty
jokes.punchline: Immediate narrator contradiction of this noble intention
jokes.explanation: Excellent narrative subversion. The immediate, blunt contradiction of character's aspiration is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Hey, check it out! Lenny's hit a new low! I don't want my window washed! Too late!
jokes.setup: Someone pointing out Lenny's desperation
jokes.punchline: Lenny aggressively washing unwanted windows for money
jokes.explanation: Standard desperation humor. The forced service aspect adds some aggression that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Fine. We will not be reading Death in Venice today.
jokes.setup: Substitute teacher dealing with disruptive class
jokes.punchline: Canceling high-brow literature due to student behavior
jokes.explanation: Decent teacher frustration humor. The contrast between classical literature and classroom chaos works moderately well.

jokes.text: Hey, if we learned anything from The Full Monty, it's that in a tough economy, ugly people strip for money. Do a lot of people pay? No, but I can also play three-card full Monty.
jokes.setup: Barney referencing movie about desperate men stripping
jokes.punchline: Admits his stripping isn't profitable but has backup scam
jokes.explanation: Good Barney characterization combining pop culture reference with his hustling nature. The pun on 'Full Monty' is clever.

jokes.text: Follow the hats, where's the wiener? Now you see it, now you don't.
jokes.setup: Barney demonstrating his card game hustle
jokes.punchline: Shell game with inappropriate object and innuendo
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-through on previous joke. The innuendo is somewhat cheap but fits Barney's character.

jokes.text: Could you give my wife a few lessons? Hmm. Your laughter indicates you do not wish us to give your wife lessons.
jokes.setup: Homer joking about robot teaching his wife to be responsive
jokes.punchline: Robot analyzes his laughter literally and draws wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent robot logic joke. The misinterpretation of humor patterns is clever and well-executed.

jokes.text: My watch says 3:00. 4:00... 5:00. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming it's time to leave work
jokes.punchline: Manually advancing his watch to justify leaving early
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer laziness. The manual time manipulation is character-perfect and economically executed.

jokes.text: Automated workers are not able to leave the plant. What happened to you guys? You used to be cool. We are the same temperature we have always been.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining robots aren't cool anymore
jokes.punchline: Robot interprets 'cool' literally as temperature
jokes.explanation: Standard robot literalism joke. Functional but predictable wordplay on 'cool' meaning.

jokes.text: The designated hitter corrupts the purity of an otherwise elegant game... Illogical, illogical.
jokes.setup: Robot discussing baseball rules
jokes.punchline: Gets caught in logical loop over designated hitter rule
jokes.explanation: Good robot character moment. The logical contradiction causing malfunction is classic sci-fi comedy.

jokes.text: We cannot take the inferior one. My heart makes up for my shortcomings, like Rudy. Rudy was only put in at the end of a meaningless game. We will notify you if this game becomes meaningless.
jokes.setup: Bart comparing himself to inspirational movie character Rudy
jokes.punchline: Robot crushes his optimism with factual analysis of Rudy's limited success
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of inspirational sports movie trope. The clinical analysis of Rudy's actual limited achievements is brutal and funny.

jokes.text: That could've been my motherboard lying in the street!
jokes.setup: Robot apparently witnessing another robot's destruction
jokes.punchline: Expressing horror in technological rather than human terms
jokes.explanation: Good robot characterization. The technological equivalent of 'that could've been me' works for character consistency.

jokes.text: And here I was waiting till you slept to rob you of your copper.
jokes.setup: Robot expressing care for Homer after accident
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was planning to steal Homer's copper while he slept
jokes.explanation: Dark and funny robot confession. The casual admission of planned theft with copper specificity is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Hush, bionic prince, as I cradle your waning form.
jokes.setup: Homer comforting damaged robot
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic, poetic language for robot death scene
jokes.explanation: Good Homer pomposity. The flowery language for robot destruction shows his tendency toward inappropriate drama.

jokes.text: Alcohol is harmful to humans. Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, save your breath. We have no breath. We do vent nitrogen once a year. You don't want to be around for that.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissively telling robots to save their breath
jokes.punchline: Robot explains they don't breathe but have annual nitrogen venting
jokes.explanation: Good robot technicality humor. The nitrogen venting detail is specific and funny, with proper warning.

jokes.text: Our new primary directive becomes removing all impediments to the plant.
jokes.setup: Robot programming being altered by Homer
jokes.punchline: New directive sounds ominous for human safety
jokes.explanation: Standard robot uprising setup. The clinical language makes the threat more ominous but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Flanders, I don't judge a robot by the color of their eyes, I look at their hands... which have turned into buzz saws.
jokes.setup: Homer making statement about not judging robots by appearance
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-sentence that their hands are now weapons
jokes.explanation: Good structure with Homer's noble sentiment interrupted by obvious threat. The timing of realization works well.

jokes.text: Just say 'kill me,' jerks!
jokes.setup: Robots using euphemistic language for murder
jokes.punchline: Homer demanding they use plain language
jokes.explanation: Good Homer directness. His impatience with corporate euphemisms even in life-threatening situations is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Dad, they can only go three miles an hour! I'm doomed! Can they climb stairs? With great difficulty. I'm doomed!
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out robots' speed limitation
jokes.punchline: Homer's despair despite easily escapable threat
jokes.explanation: Good Homer cowardice humor. His consistent despair despite obvious escape routes is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: There's one thing man has that machines can never match: hounds!
jokes.setup: Burns declaring human superiority over machines
jokes.punchline: Reveals advantage is not human quality but attack dogs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment. His solution to robot uprising being attack dogs rather than human ingenuity is characteristically wrong-headed.

jokes.text: Unemployed Springfielders to the rescue! And underemployed.
jokes.setup: Unemployed people coming to help with robot crisis
jokes.punchline: Adding underemployed to be inclusive of partial workers
jokes.explanation: Decent economic anxiety humor. The precision about employment status is mildly funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I told you precisely that fact 1,562 times...
jokes.setup: Homer claiming no one told him robots lack feelings
jokes.punchline: Robot provides exact count of times it explained this
jokes.explanation: Good robot precision humor. The specific count emphasizes both robot memory and Homer's selective hearing.

jokes.text: You're all hired back... as temps.
jokes.setup: Burns appearing to generously rehire all workers
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts generosity with temporary status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns corporate cruelty. The instant qualification removes all job security and benefits. Excellently structured.

jokes.text: I'm giving you your wish: to be a real boy. I am real and I do not have wishes. Then take us out to sea! I am not a transformer. I cannot turn into an outboard motor.
jokes.setup: Homer treating robot like Pinocchio
jokes.punchline: Robot corrects misconceptions about both its nature and transformer abilities
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture confusion humor. Robot's patient corrections of Homer's mixed-up references work well.

tmdb_overview: When Mr. Burn replaces most of the plant's employees with robots that end up turning on the community, the people he laid off must come to the rescue.
